title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery Quin, Professor Yadley, and Yale discuss the murder of Braid. Ellery deduces that the killer, Crosetti, must have been someone Braid knew well, regardless of any disguise used, because Braid would not have invited a complete stranger into his unprotected home to play chess. Based on this, Ellery realizes that Crosetti is not actually a cripple; rather, he used a childhood disability as a consistent physical feature to mislead the police, abandoning the limp once he sensed danger.

Ellery then reviews the sequence of crimes, noting that the imposter Crocetta killed the first victim, Clinton, due to a family feud, mistakenly believing he was killing Andrew. After discovering a note from Braid indicating that Van (Tervil) was still alive, Crocetta's motive shifted toward finding Van to complete his revenge. Ellery realizes that Croasdale has been waiting for the opportunity to find Van through Megalas. He concludes that Croasdale intends to kill Megalas once he obtains this information.

Realizing the imminent danger, Ellery urgently sends a telegram to the Sheriff of Aroon, West Virginia, instructing him to organize a force to guard Old Pete's shack and keep Old Pete safe until Ellery arrives. He also requests that the sheriff report Croasdale's return to Crummett. Ellery attempts to contact Officer Vaughn to coordinate further, but learns that Vaughn and District Attorney Eisam have already driven off in a car after receiving a telegram.
